title: Marketing Lead — Medium Business
description: You're the Marketing Lead at a growing Australian business. You might manage 1-2 junior team members or be a senior individual contributor. You do both strategy and execution — planning campaigns in the morning and writing email copy in the afternoon. It's hands-on, fast-paced, and you see the direct impact of your work.

schedule.activity: Check email, Slack, and social media notifications. Respond to any urgent items from overnight.
Quick sync with the CEO or Head of Sales on this week's marketing priorities
schedule.note: In medium businesses, marketing often reports directly to the CEO

schedule.activity: Write and schedule this week's social media content
schedule.note: You're creating the content yourself, not just approving it

schedule.activity: Deep work: Build a landing page for the upcoming product feature launch
Lunch — sometimes at desk while reviewing campaign analytics
Review Google Ads and Meta Ads performance, adjust budgets and bids
Write email newsletter for this week — including featured product, blog recap, and CTA
Briefing call with freelance designer about new campaign creative
SEO check: review Search Console data, update keyword tracking, brief a blog article
Meet with Sales to review lead quality from recent campaigns
schedule.note: Feedback loop with Sales is critical for improving targeting

schedule.activity: Plan next month's campaign calendar and content themes
Wrap up: update project board, set tomorrow's top 3 priorities
Head home — occasionally check social media engagement in the evening

responsibilities: Own the entire marketing function across all channels
Create and execute content (social, email, blog, ads)
Build and optimise the lead generation funnel
Manage 1-2 junior team members or freelancers
Maintain the website and landing pages
Coordinate with Sales on lead quality and pipeline
challenges: Wearing many hats — strategy AND execution every day
Limited budget compared to larger competitors
Being the only senior marketing person (lonely at the top)
Pressure to deliver measurable results quickly
Keeping up with multiple channels simultaneously
Balancing quality with speed when you're the one producing everything
rewards: Direct impact — you can see the results of your work immediately
Broad skill development across the entire marketing mix
Close relationship with leadership and influence on company direction
Autonomy to make decisions without layers of approval
Fast career growth as the company scales
Great preparation for a senior role at a larger company or starting your own business
careerPath: VP Marketing or CMO at a larger company, or start your own business
